questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: Let $f(x)=12 x^2 \int_0^1 y f(y) d y+20 \int_0^1 x y^2 f(y) d y+4 x$Question: The maximum value of $f(x)$ is
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { Let } 12 \int_0^1 y f(y) d y=a \text { and } 20 \int_0^1\left(y^2 f(y)\right) d y+4=b \\
& \Rightarrow f(x)=a x^2+b x \Rightarrow f(y)=a y^2+b y \\
& \Rightarrow f(x)=12 x^2 \int_0^1 y\left(a y^2+b y\right) d y+20 x \int_0^1 y^2\left(a y^2+b y\right) d y+4 x=12 x^2\left(\frac{a}{4}+\frac{b}{3}\right)+20 x\left[\frac{a}{5}+\frac{b}{4}\right]+4 x \\
& \Rightarrow a=3 a+4 b \Rightarrow a=-2 b ...(1) \\
& a n d 4 a+5 b+4=b \Rightarrow a+b=-1 ...(2)\\
& \Rightarrow a=-2, b=1 \\
& \therefore f(x)=-2 x^2+x
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph:$f(x)$ satisfies the relation $f(x)-\lambda \int_0^{\pi / 2} \sin x \cos t f(t) d t=\sin x$Question:If $\int_0^{\pi / 2} f(x) d x=3$, then the value of $\lambda$ is
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & f(x)-\lambda \sin x \int_0^{\pi / 2} \operatorname{cost} f(t) d t=\sin x \\ & \Rightarrow f(x)-P \sin x=\sin x \\ & \text { or } f(x)=(P+1) \sin x \\ & \text { Where } P=\lambda \int_0^{\pi / 2} \cos t f(t) d t \\ & =\lambda \int_0^{\pi / 2} \operatorname{cost}(P+1) \sin t d t \\ & =\frac{\lambda(P+1)}{2} \int_0^{\pi / 2} \sin 2 t d t \\ & P=\frac{\lambda(P+1)}{2} \Rightarrow P=\frac{\lambda}{2-\lambda} \\ & f(x)=\left(\frac{\lambda}{2-\lambda}+1\right) \sin x=2 \\ & \Rightarrow \sin x=2-\lambda \\ & \Rightarrow \lambda \in[1,3] \\ & \int_0^{\pi / 2} \frac{2}{2-\lambda} \sin x d x=3 \\ & \Rightarrow \lambda=\frac{4}{3}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f$ and $g$ be two real valued differentiable function on $R$ satisfying $\int_0^x g(t) d t=3 x+\int_x^0 \cos ^2 t \cdot g(t) d t$ and $f(x)=\lim _{\alpha \rightarrow 0} \frac{1}{\alpha^4} \int_0^\alpha \frac{\left(e^{x+t}-e^x\right) \ln ^2(1+t)}{2 t^3+3} d t$Question: f(ln 2) is greater than
questions.solution:
We have $f(x)=\lim _{\alpha \rightarrow 0} \frac{e^x}{\alpha^4} \int_0^\alpha \frac{\left(e^t-1\right) \ln ^2(1+t)}{\left(2 t^3+3\right)} d t\left[\frac{0}{0}\right.$ form $]$
$\begin{aligned}
& =\lim _{\alpha \rightarrow 0} \frac{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}\left(\mathrm{e}^\alpha-1\right) \ln ^2(1+\alpha)}{\left(2 \alpha^3+3\right) 4 \alpha^3} \\
& =\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}} \lim _{\alpha \rightarrow 0} \frac{\mathrm{e}^\alpha-1}{\alpha} \cdot \frac{\ln ^2(1+\alpha)}{\alpha^2} \cdot \frac{1}{4\left(2 \alpha^3+3\right)} \\
& =\frac{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}}{12}
\end{aligned}$
Clearly $f(\ln 2)=\frac{e^{\ln 2}}{12}=\frac{1}{6}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: Read the following write up carefully and answer the following questions: Let $f(x)=12 x^2 \int_0^1 y f(y) d y+20 \int_0^1 x y^2 f(y) d y+4 x$Question: The number of solutions of the equation $|f(|x|)|=e^{|x|}$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { Let } 12 \int_0^1 y f(y) d y=a \text { and } 20 \int_0^1\left(y^2 f(y)\right) d y+4=b \\
& \Rightarrow f(x)=a x^2+b x \Rightarrow f(y)=a y^2+b y \\
& \Rightarrow f(x)=12 x^2 \int_0^1 y\left(a y^2+b y\right) d y+20 x \int_0^1 y^2\left(a y^2+b y\right) d y+4 x=12 x^2\left(\frac{a}{4}+\frac{b}{3}\right)+20 x\left[\frac{a}{5}+\frac{b}{4}\right]+4 x \\
& \Rightarrow a=3 a+4 b \Rightarrow a=-2 b ...(1) \\
& a n d 4 a+5 b+4=b \Rightarrow a+b=-1 ...(2)\\
& \Rightarrow a=-2, b=1 \\
& \therefore f(x)=-2 x^2+x
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: Paragraph: $f(x)$ satisfies the relation $f(x)-\lambda \int_0^{\pi / 2} \sin x \cos t f(t) d t=\sin x$Question: If $f(x)=2$ has at least one real root, then
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & f(x)-\lambda \sin x \int_0^{\pi / 2} \operatorname{cost} f(t) d t=\sin x \\ & \Rightarrow f(x)-P \sin x=\sin x \\ & \text { or } f(x)=(P+1) \sin x \\ & \text { Where } P=\lambda \int_0^{\pi / 2} \cos t f(t) d t \\ & =\lambda \int_0^{\pi / 2} \operatorname{cost}(P+1) \sin t d t \\ & =\frac{\lambda(P+1)}{2} \int_0^{\pi / 2} \sin 2 t d t \\ & P=\frac{\lambda(P+1)}{2} \Rightarrow P=\frac{\lambda}{2-\lambda} \\ & f(x)=\left(\frac{\lambda}{2-\lambda}+1\right) \sin x=2 \\ & \Rightarrow \sin x=2-\lambda \\ & \Rightarrow \lambda \in[1,3] \\ & \int_0^{\pi / 2} \frac{2}{2-\lambda} \sin x d x=3 \\ & \Rightarrow \lambda=\frac{4}{3}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f$ and $g$ be two real valued differentiable function on $R$ satisfying $\int_0^x g(t) d t=3 x+\int_x^0 \cos ^2 t \cdot g(t) d t$ and $f(x)=\lim _{\alpha \rightarrow 0} \frac{1}{\alpha^4} \int_0^\alpha \frac{\left(e^{x+t}-e^x\right) \ln ^2(1+t)}{2 t^3+3} d t$Question: Range of g(x) is equal to
questions.solution:
We have $\int_0^x g(t) d t=3 x+\int_x^0 \cos ^2 t \cdot g(t) d t$
On differentiating both side w.r.t $x$
$\begin{aligned}
& g(x)=3-\cos ^2 x g(x) \\
& g(x)=\frac{3}{1+\cos ^2 x}
\end{aligned}$
Clearly $\frac{3}{2} \leq \frac{3}{1+\cos ^2 x} \leq 3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f$ and $g$ be two real valued differentiable function on $R$ satisfying $\int_0^x g(t) d t=3 x+\int_x^0 \cos ^2 t \cdot g(t) d t$ and $f(x)=\lim _{\alpha \rightarrow 0} \frac{1}{\alpha^4} \int_0^\alpha \frac{\left(e^{x+t}-e^x\right) \ln ^2(1+t)}{2 t^3+3} d t$Question: The value of definite integral $\int_0^{\pi / 2} g(x) d x$ has in the interval
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \text { Let } I=\int_0^{\pi / 2} g(x) d x \\ & =\int_0^{\pi / 2} \frac{3}{1+\cos ^2 x} d x \\ & =\int_0^{\pi / 2} \frac{3 \sec ^2 x}{\tan ^2 x+2} d x \\ & =\frac{3 \pi}{2 \sqrt{2}}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: If $\mathrm{m}\gt0, \mathrm{n}\gt0$, the definite integral $\mathrm{I}=\int^1 \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{m}-1}(1-\mathrm{x})^{\mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{dx}$ depends upon the values of m and n and is denoted by $\beta(\mathrm{m}, \mathrm{n})$, called the beta function. e.g. $\int_0^1 x^4(1-x)^5 d x=\int_0^1 x^{5-1}(1-x)^{6-1} d x=\beta(5,6)$ and $\int_0^1 x^{5 / 2}(1-x)^{-1 / 2} d x=\int_0^1 x^{7 / 2-1}(1-x)^{1 / 2-1} d x=\beta\left(\frac{7}{2}, \frac{1}{2}\right)$ Obviously, $\beta(\mathrm{n}, \mathrm{m})=\beta(\mathrm{m}, \mathrm{n})$.Question: The integral $\int_0^{\pi / 2} \cos ^{2 m} \theta \sin ^{2 n} \theta \mathrm{~d} \theta$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Writing $\sin ^2 \theta=\mathrm{x}$. we get
$2 \sin \theta \cos \theta \mathrm{~d} \theta=\mathrm{dx}$, and hence the given integral is equal to
$\frac{1}{2} \int_0^{\pi / 2} \cos ^{2 m-1} \sin ^{2 n-1} \theta(2 \sin \theta \cos \theta) d \theta$
$=\frac{1}{2} \int_0^1\left(\cos ^2 \theta\right)^{\frac{2 m-1}{2}}\left(\sin ^2 \theta\right)^{\frac{2 n-1}{2}} \mathrm{dx}=\frac{1}{2} \int_0^1(1-x)^{m-1 / 2} x^{n-1 / 2} d x=\frac{1}{2} \beta\left(m+\frac{1}{2}, n+\frac{1}{2}\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: If $\mathrm{m}\gt0, \mathrm{n}\gt0$, the definite integral $\mathrm{I}=\int^1 \mathrm{x}^{\mathrm{m}-1}(1-\mathrm{x})^{\mathrm{n}-1} \mathrm{dx}$ depends upon the values of m and n and is denoted by $\beta(\mathrm{m}, \mathrm{n})$, called the beta function. e.g. $\int_0^1 x^4(1-x)^5 d x=\int_0^1 x^{5-1}(1-x)^{6-1} d x=\beta(5,6)$ and $\int_0^1 x^{5 / 2}(1-x)^{-1 / 2} d x=\int_0^1 x^{7 / 2-1}(1-x)^{1 / 2-1} d x=\beta\left(\frac{7}{2}, \frac{1}{2}\right)$ Obviously, $\beta(\mathrm{n}, \mathrm{m})=\beta(\mathrm{m}, \mathrm{n})$.Question: If $\int_0^n\left(1-\frac{x}{n}\right)^n x^{k-1} d x=R \beta(k, n+1)$, then $R$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Writing $\frac{x}{n}=2$, we get
$\int_0^n\left(1-\frac{x}{n}\right)^n x^{k-1} d x=\int_0^1(1-z)^n(n z)^{k-1} n d z=n^k \int_0^1(1-z)^n z^{k-1} d z=n^k \beta(k, n+1)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A quadratic polynomial $f(x)$ with positive leading coefficient such that $g(x)=f(\ln x) \forall x>0$. Also the curve $y=g(x)$ satisfy the following condition. (a) There is exactly one value for a positive number $p$ such that $(p, g(p))$ is its extremum point and $\left(p^2, g\left(p^2\right)\right)$ is its inflection point. (b) Exactly one tangent line can be drawn from the point $(0,0)$ to the curve $y=g(x)$.Question: The value of $\frac{f(10)}{f(2)}$ equals:
questions.solution:
$$ \begin{aligned} & f(x)=a x^2+b x+c \\ & g(x)=a \ln ^2 x+b \ln x+c \forall x>0 \end{aligned} $$ Given, $g^{\prime}(p)=0$ $\Rightarrow \quad \frac{2 a \ln p+b}{p}=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \ln p=\frac{-b}{2 a}$ Also $g^{\prime \prime}\left(p^2\right)=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \frac{2 a\left(1-\ln p^2\right)-b}{p^4}=0$ $$ \Rightarrow \quad 1-\frac{b}{2 a}=\ln p^2 $$ $$ \therefore \quad 1-\frac{b}{2 a}=2 \ln p=\frac{-b}{a} $$ $\therefore \quad b=-2 a$ Also,
$$ \begin{aligned} & g^{\prime}(\alpha)=\frac{g(\alpha)-0}{\alpha-0} \\ & \frac{2 a \ln \alpha+b}{\alpha}=\frac{a \ln ^2 \alpha+b \ln \alpha+c}{\alpha} \\ & a \ln ^2 \alpha+(b-2 a) \ln \alpha+c-b=0 \end{aligned} $$ For unique $\alpha, D=0$ $$ \begin{aligned} & (b-2 a)^2-4 a(c-b)=0 \\ & 4 b^2=4 a(c-b) \\ & 4 a=c+2 a \\ & \therefore \quad c=2 a \\ & \therefore \quad f(x)=a\left(x^2-2 x+2\right) \end{aligned} $$ Now, proceed.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: A quadratic polynomial $f(x)$ with positive leading coefficient such that $g(x)=f(\ln x) \forall x>0$. Also the curve $y=g(x)$ satisfy the following condition. (a) There is exactly one value for a positive number $p$ such that $(p, g(p))$ is its extremum point and $\left(p^2, g\left(p^2\right)\right)$ is its inflection point. (b) Exactly one tangent line can be drawn from the point $(0,0)$ to the curve $y=g(x)$.Question: The value of definite integral of $\int_1^2 \frac{f(0)}{f(x)} d x$ equals:
questions.solution:
$$ \begin{aligned} & f(x)=a x^2+b x+c \\ & g(x)=a \ln ^2 x+b \ln x+c \forall x>0 \end{aligned} $$ Given, $g^{\prime}(p)=0$ $\Rightarrow \quad \frac{2 a \ln p+b}{p}=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \ln p=\frac{-b}{2 a}$ Also $g^{\prime \prime}\left(p^2\right)=0 \quad \Rightarrow \quad \frac{2 a\left(1-\ln p^2\right)-b}{p^4}=0$ $$ \Rightarrow \quad 1-\frac{b}{2 a}=\ln p^2 $$ $$ \therefore \quad 1-\frac{b}{2 a}=2 \ln p=\frac{-b}{a} $$ $\therefore \quad b=-2 a$ Also,
$$ \begin{aligned} & g^{\prime}(\alpha)=\frac{g(\alpha)-0}{\alpha-0} \\ & \frac{2 a \ln \alpha+b}{\alpha}=\frac{a \ln ^2 \alpha+b \ln \alpha+c}{\alpha} \\ & a \ln ^2 \alpha+(b-2 a) \ln \alpha+c-b=0 \end{aligned} $$ For unique $\alpha, D=0$ $$ \begin{aligned} & (b-2 a)^2-4 a(c-b)=0 \\ & 4 b^2=4 a(c-b) \\ & 4 a=c+2 a \\ & \therefore \quad c=2 a \\ & \therefore \quad f(x)=a\left(x^2-2 x+2\right) \end{aligned} $$ Now, proceed.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f(x)$ be a polynomial of degree 3 such that $f(0)=1, f(1)=2$ and zero is a critical point of $f(x)$ having no local extreme.Question: If the value of definite integral $\int_{-1}^1 \frac{f(x)}{\sqrt{x^2+7}} d x$ is equal to $2 \ln \left(\frac{\sqrt{a}+1}{\sqrt{b}+c}\right)$ then the value of $(a+b+c)$, is:
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{array}{lll}
f^{\prime}(x)=3 a x^2 & \Rightarrow & f(x)=a x^3+b \\
f(0)=b=1 & \text { and } & f(1)=a+1=2 \quad \Rightarrow \quad a=1
\end{array}
$$

Hence, $f(x)=x^3+1$
(i) Limit $=e^{\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{x^3}{\tan x-x}}=e^3$
(ii)
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { D.I. }=\int_{-1}^1 \frac{x^3+1}{\sqrt{x^2+7}} d x=2 \int_0^1 \frac{1}{\sqrt{x^2+7}} d x=2\left(\ln \left(x+\sqrt{x^2+7}\right)\right)_0^1=2 \ln \left(\frac{\sqrt{8}+1}{\sqrt{7}}\right) \\
& a+b+c=8+7+0=15
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $P=\int_0^1 \sqrt{\frac{x}{1-x}} \ln \left(\frac{x}{1-x}\right) d x, Q=\pi \ln \left(\frac{\sqrt{\alpha+1}+1}{2}\right)$ and $R=\int_0^8 e^{Q / P} d \alpha$.Question: The value of $P$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
$$
P=\int_0^1 \sqrt{\frac{x}{1-x}} \ln \left(\frac{x}{1-x}\right) d x
$$

Put
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \frac{x}{1-x}=t^2 \Rightarrow x=t^2-t^2 x \\
& \Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{t^2}{1+t^2}=1-\frac{1}{1+t^2} \\
& d x=\frac{2 t}{\left(1+t^2\right)^2} \\
& P=\int_0^{\infty} t(2 \ln t) \frac{2 t}{\left(1+t^2\right)^2} d t \\
& P=2 \int_0^{\infty} \underbrace{(t \ln t)}_{\text {(i) }} \underbrace{\frac{2 t}{\left(1+t^2\right)^2}}_{\text {(ii) }} d t \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
$\begin{aligned} P & =2\left[\left.(t \ln t)\left(\frac{-1}{1+t^2}\right)\right|_0 ^{\infty}+\int_0^{\infty} \frac{(1+\ln t)}{1+t^2} d t\right] \\ & =2[0+\underbrace{\tan ^{-1} t}_0]_0^{\infty}+\underbrace{\int_0^{\infty} \frac{\ln t}{1+t^2} d t}_0 \\ & =\pi \\ \text { Now, } R & =\int_0^8 \frac{\sqrt{\alpha+1}+1}{2} d_\alpha=\frac{38}{3} \\ \therefore \quad 3 R & =38\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $P=\int_0^1 \sqrt{\frac{x}{1-x}} \ln \left(\frac{x}{1-x}\right) d x, Q=\pi \ln \left(\frac{\sqrt{\alpha+1}+1}{2}\right)$ and $R=\int_0^8 e^{Q / P} d \alpha$.Question: The value of $3 R$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
$$
P=\int_0^1 \sqrt{\frac{x}{1-x}} \ln \left(\frac{x}{1-x}\right) d x
$$

Put
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \frac{x}{1-x}=t^2 \Rightarrow x=t^2-t^2 x \\
& \Rightarrow \quad x=\frac{t^2}{1+t^2}=1-\frac{1}{1+t^2} \\
& d x=\frac{2 t}{\left(1+t^2\right)^2} \\
& P=\int_0^{\infty} t(2 \ln t) \frac{2 t}{\left(1+t^2\right)^2} d t \\
& P=2 \int_0^{\infty} \underbrace{(t \ln t)}_{\text {(i) }} \underbrace{\frac{2 t}{\left(1+t^2\right)^2}}_{\text {(ii) }} d t \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
$\begin{aligned} P & =2\left[\left.(t \ln t)\left(\frac{-1}{1+t^2}\right)\right|_0 ^{\infty}+\int_0^{\infty} \frac{(1+\ln t)}{1+t^2} d t\right] \\ & =2[0+\underbrace{\tan ^{-1} t}_0]_0^{\infty}+\underbrace{\int_0^{\infty} \frac{\ln t}{1+t^2} d t}_0 \\ & =\pi \\ \text { Now, } R & =\int_0^8 \frac{\sqrt{\alpha+1}+1}{2} d_\alpha=\frac{38}{3} \\ \therefore \quad 3 R & =38\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f$ be a continuous function such that $g(x)=\int_{-1}^1 f(t)|x-t| d t$ where $x \in(-1,1)$.Question: If $f(x)=x^2$, then the value of $g^{\prime}(1)$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
For $x \in(-1,1)$
$$
g(x)=\int_{-1}^x f(t)(x-t) d t+\int_x^1 f(t)(t-x) d t=x \int_{-1}^x f(t) d t-\int_{-1}^x t f(t) d t+\int_x^1 t f(t) d t-x \int_x^1 f(t) d t
$$

Now, differentiate
$$
\begin{aligned}
& g^{\prime}(x)=\int_{-1}^x f(t) d t+x \frac{d}{d x} \int_{-1}^x f(t) d t-\frac{d}{d x} \int_{-1}^x t f(t) d t+\frac{d}{d x} \int_x^1 t f(t) d t-\int_x^1 f(t) d t-x \frac{d}{d x} \int_x^1 f(t) d t \\
& g^{\prime}(x)=\int_{-1}^x f(t) d t+x f(x)-f(x) x-f(x) x-f(x) x-\int_x^1 f(t) d t+x f(x) \\
& g^{\prime}(x)=\int_{-1}^x f(t) d t-\int_x^1 f(t) d t .... (1)
\end{aligned}
$$

Again differentiate
$$
g^{\prime \prime}(x)=\frac{d}{d x} \int_{-1}^x f(t) d t-\frac{d}{d x} \int_x^1 f(t) d t=f(x)+f(x)=2 f(x) .... (2)
$$

Now, verify alternatives
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Paragraph: Let $f$ be a continuous function such that $g(x)=\int_{-1}^1 f(t)|x-t| d t$ where $x \in(-1,1)$.Question: The value of $\int_0^1 \frac{g^{\prime \prime}(x)}{f(x)} d x$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
For $x \in(-1,1)$
$$
g(x)=\int_{-1}^x f(t)(x-t) d t+\int_x^1 f(t)(t-x) d t=x \int_{-1}^x f(t) d t-\int_{-1}^x t f(t) d t+\int_x^1 t f(t) d t-x \int_x^1 f(t) d t
$$

Now, differentiate
$$
\begin{aligned}
& g^{\prime}(x)=\int_{-1}^x f(t) d t+x \frac{d}{d x} \int_{-1}^x f(t) d t-\frac{d}{d x} \int_{-1}^x t f(t) d t+\frac{d}{d x} \int_x^1 t f(t) d t-\int_x^1 f(t) d t-x \frac{d}{d x} \int_x^1 f(t) d t \\
& g^{\prime}(x)=\int_{-1}^x f(t) d t+x f(x)-f(x) x-f(x) x-f(x) x-\int_x^1 f(t) d t+x f(x) \\
& g^{\prime}(x)=\int_{-1}^x f(t) d t-\int_x^1 f(t) d t .... (1)
\end{aligned}
$$

Again differentiate
$$
g^{\prime \prime}(x)=\frac{d}{d x} \int_{-1}^x f(t) d t-\frac{d}{d x} \int_x^1 f(t) d t=f(x)+f(x)=2 f(x) .... (2)
$$

Now, verify alternatives
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
